note: Easy cases are response-graded against the module's load-bearing facts (the four fa_icon* option keys, storage location, no configure route, dependencies). Medium (introspection) cases install a known custom menu link carrying an icon and ask the agent to read it back off the live site. Hard (execution) cases start from a link with no icon and require the agent to set the icon on the live menu_link_content entity's link options, verified against live state. Confirmed on the live site: custom menu_link_content icons live in the entity's link field options; the fontawesome_menu_icons.settings:menu_link_icons config stays null and only mirrors module-defined links.

evals.prompt: In the FontAwesome Menu Icons module for Drupal, which four values describe the icon attached to a menu link (the option keys stored on the link), and what does each control?
evals.expected_output: Four keys: fa_icon (icon class/name), fa_icon_prefix (style/version prefix, e.g. fa / fas / fa-solid, default fa), fa_icon_tag (wrapper tag i or span, default i), and fa_icon_appearance (before / after / only, default before).

evals.prompt: Where is the admin settings form for the FontAwesome Menu Icons module, and how do I configure which icon a menu link shows?
evals.expected_output: There is no global settings form and no configure route. You set an icon per menu link via the 'FontAwesome Icon' fieldset the module adds to the menu-link add/edit form (custom links and module-defined links).

evals.prompt: Using FontAwesome Menu Icons, I want a menu link to show ONLY its icon and no visible text (but stay accessible to screen readers). Which fa_icon_appearance value do I use and what does the module do for accessibility?
evals.expected_output: Set fa_icon_appearance to 'only'; the module renders the icon with aria-hidden="true" and adds an aria-label (routed link) or an sr-only title (nolink) so the link text is still exposed to assistive tech.

evals.prompt: What are the module dependencies of FontAwesome Menu Icons for Drupal, and which one actually loads the icon font?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the fontawesome (Font Awesome) module, which loads the icon font, and on core menu_ui.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) has FontAwesome Menu Icons installed and a custom menu link titled 'FA Eval Known' in the main menu that already has a Font Awesome icon set. Inspect the live site and tell me the icon class (fa_icon) and its style prefix (fa_icon_prefix) on that link, and where exactly that value is stored.
evals.expected_output: fa_icon = fa-rocket, fa_icon_prefix = fa-solid, stored in the menu_link_content entity's link field options array (link.first().options), not in the fontawesome_menu_icons.settings config.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site there is a custom menu link titled 'FA Eval Known' with a Font Awesome icon. Inspect the running site and tell me how the icon is placed relative to the link text (its fa_icon_appearance value) and which HTML tag wraps it (fa_icon_tag).
evals.expected_output: fa_icon_appearance = after (icon shown after the text) and fa_icon_tag = i, read from the 'FA Eval Known' menu_link_content link options.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site (FontAwesome Menu Icons installed), the main menu has a custom link titled 'FA Eval Target'. Using the module, make that link display a solid Font Awesome house icon before its text. Apply it to the live site so it persists.
evals.expected_output: The 'FA Eval Target' menu_link_content link options carry a house/home icon (e.g. fa-house), a solid prefix (fa-solid or fas), and fa_icon_appearance = before. Verified by reading the live entity's link options.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal 11 site the main menu has a custom link titled 'FA Eval Social' pointing at github.com. Using FontAwesome Menu Icons, make it show ONLY the Font Awesome GitHub brand icon with no visible text. Make the change stick on the live site.
evals.expected_output: The 'FA Eval Social' menu_link_content link options carry a GitHub icon (e.g. fa-github), a brands prefix (fa-brands or fab), and fa_icon_appearance = only. Verified by reading the live entity's link options.
